summary:
Not all mature proteins start with methionine because the initiating methionine may be removed during post-translational modification. While methionine initiates translation via the AUG codon, its absence from certain mature proteins makes this claim incorrect for end products. 

The summary above contains redundant information; please revise only what needs revision while preserving factual accuracy.

Revised Summary:

Not all mature proteins start with methionine since the initiator methionine can be cleaved off after translation. This post-translational processing means that even though most polypeptide chains initiate with methionine, they don't always remain so in their final form.
